issue.message: Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Implicitly using the default locale is a common source of bugs: Use `String.format(Locale, ...)` instead
Requiring Wifi permissions limits app availability on TVs that support only Ethernet

`controlReceiver` is missing `RECEIVER_EXPORTED` or `RECEIVER_NOT_EXPORTED` flag for unprotected broadcasts registered for com.playbridge.player.ACTION_CONTROL, com.playbridge.player.ACTION_REMOTE, com.playbridge.player.ACTION_PLAY, com.playbridge.player.ACTION_QUEUE_ADD, com.playbridge.player.ACTION_PLAYLIST_JUMP, com.playbridge.player.ACTION_RESYNC
`remoteReceiver` is missing `RECEIVER_EXPORTED` or `RECEIVER_NOT_EXPORTED` flag for unprotected broadcasts registered for com.playbridge.player.ACTION_REMOTE
`contextIdleReceiver` is missing `RECEIVER_EXPORTED` or `RECEIVER_NOT_EXPORTED` flag for unprotected broadcasts registered for com.playbridge.player.ACTION_CONTEXT_IDLE
A newer version of Gradle than 9.1.0 is available: 9.5.1
A newer version of androidx.dynamicanimation:dynamicanimation than 1.0.0 is available: 1.1.0
A newer version of org.java-websocket:Java-WebSocket than 1.5.7 is available: 1.6.0
A newer version of org.bouncycastle:bcpkix-jdk15to18 than 1.78.1 is available: 1.84

`checkClientTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers
issue.errorLine1: override fun checkClientTrusted(chain: Array<out java.security.cert.X509Certificate>?, authType: String?) {}

issue.message: `checkServerTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers
issue.errorLine1: override fun checkServerTrusted(chain: Array<out java.security.cert.X509Certificate>?, authType: String?) {}

issue.message: `checkServerTrusted` is empty, which could cause insecure network traffic due to trusting arbitrary TLS/SSL certificates presented by peers

Implementing a custom `X509TrustManager` is error-prone and likely to be insecure. It is likely to disable certificate validation altogether, and is non-trivial to implement correctly without calling Android's default implementation.
Insecure Base Configuration
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 24
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
Unnecessary; `SDK_INT` is always >= 26
This folder configuration (`v26`) is unnecessary; `minSdkVersion` is 26. Merge all the resources in this folder into `mipmap-anydpi`.
Do not place Android context classes in static fields (static reference to `AdBlocker` which has field `context` pointing to `Context`); this is a memory leak
Possible overdraw: Root element paints background `@android:color/black` with a theme that also paints a background (inferred theme is `@android_style/Theme_NoTitleBar_Fullscreen`)
Possible overdraw: Root element paints background `@android:color/black` with a theme that also paints a background (inferred theme is `@android_style/Theme_NoTitleBar_Fullscreen`)

String literal in `setText` can not be translated. Use Android resources instead.

String literal in `setText` can not be translated. Use Android resources instead.
Do not concatenate text displayed with `setText`. Use resource string with placeholders.

String literal in `setText` can not be translated. Use Android resources instead.
